name: Assumption Cultural Center
description: Assumption Cultural Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in St. Clair Shores, MI. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Amenities include restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and trainers or lessons. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Avis Park
description: Avis Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Sterling Heights, MI. There are 2 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: Bash Pickleball Club
description:
Booking: Court Reserve app required.

Membership: Optional for open play/drop-in; impacts cost.

Open Play: Free with membership; mixed skills (beginner to advanced beginner).

Drop-In: Fee-based; $5 member discount; skilled players.

name: Shelby Township Senior Center
description: Shelby Township Senior Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Shelby, MI. There are 7 concrete and wood courts. 4 are outdoors and 3 are indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and locker rooms. You can reserve the courts. The courts are free.
open_play_schedule: Outdoor courts are open anytime except Wednesday evenings for league play. Several indoor pickleball sessions are scheduled each day for Seniors. You must sign up online in order to guarantee a spot. Additional indoor sessions are available for online sign-up through the Parks & Rec Dept. in the evenings and on the weekends.

name: The BRAC
description: The 2 side-by-side courts are located in the gym of the Brother Robert Activity Center and are available from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Monday through Friday. Weekends are also available on a first-come, first-served basis. Pickleballs and paddles will also be available.
